visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The visa for a family reunification with a German or EU citizen is given in request for a national visa in order to join husband, wife or partner who is a national of Germany or the European
                                                                        Union and who lives in Germany.
You must submit your visa application with prior appointment at VFS Global.

## Visa Fees
VFS Global is collecting three types of application fees:
- Visa fees, which are collected on behalf of the consular authority for the application processing.
- Application Service fees, which correspond to the standard application processing service provided by VFS Global.
- Added Value Service fees, which correspond to optional added value services you have used during the application process.
Attention: The payment of the above fees does not guarantee that a visa will be granted. The visa fee and the service fee are both non-refundable and non-transferable after the application is transferred to the consular authority, the visa application is denied by the administration, or the applicant decides to terminate his or her application.
Effective Date 30 March 2026

Visa fees

[Table:  Reunification with Spouse or Partner]
The fee is fixed in Euro but payable in Thai Baht only.
The exchange rate between EUR and THB is decided by the Consular Authority and updated periodically. The applicable visa and service fees in THB depend on the current exchange rate, which can be changed without any notifications.
Visa applications for spouses, registered civil partners and underage unmarried children of Germans, as well as parents of underage Germans are processed free of charge. Visa applications for spouses entitled to freedom of movement and children of EU/EEA nationals are also free of charge.
Application service fee
The below service fee is mandatory for all visa applications and is inclusive of taxes. The service fee is to be paid for every visa application. This fee corresponds to the standard application processing service provided by VFS Global (i.e., information provision, appointment booking, visa application reception, data processing, etc.).

[Table:  Reunification with Spouse or Partner]

[Table:  Reunification with Spouse or Partner]
Value Added Service fees
Please note that Value Added Services can be bought at the sole discretion of the applicant and do not have any effect on the visa decision. For more information, please check the Value Added Service page.
Note:
An official receipt should be obtained for all payments made.
Kindly be informed that the fees are non-refundable and non-transferable once the payment is made.

## Documents Required
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application at VFS Global in Bangkok; this will minimize chances of rejection of your visa application
                                                                        due to incorrect or inadequate documents.
Visabestimmungen (Deutsch) – Nationales Visum zum Ehegattennachzug / Nachzug zum Lebenspartner (https://bangkok.diplo.de/th-de/service/05-VisaEinreise/visum-zum-ehegattennachzug-nachzug-zum-lebenspartner/1603978)
The Embassy of Germany may request additional documents or information, according to your situation.
Non-EU/EFTA nationals must have sufficient financial resources for the entire stay, assessed by the length and purpose of the visit.

## Photo Specifications
Please submit two passport photos which should fulfill the following criteria (conform to ICAO standards).
The photos are part of the biometric data, mandatory for a Schengen visa application.
- Colour photo on a white background
- Size: 3.5 x 4.5cm
- Taken from the front with the subject looking directly at the camera
- Mouth must be closed, teeth not visible
- Without shadows in the face
- Wearer of glasses: the eyes must not be covered by the frames of the glasses
- Sharp, not blurred, high pixel resolution (600 DPI)
- Face covers 70-80% of the photo
- Recent photo, not older than 6 months
The photo will be imprinted into the visa sticker and must fulfill these criteria. The photo is stored in different databases (central register for foreigners, visa information system),
                                                                        so that a comparison can take place when entering the Schengen area.
Photos which are older than 6 months are not accepted by the visa section and applications containing photographs that are older than 6 months will be rejected and not processed. In order
                                                                        to avoid this, we advise you to take new photos right before the visa application submission.

## Processing Time
As long-term visas can only be issued after approval by the competent Aliens’ Office in Germany and due to long mailing times, applications take two months on average.

- Researchers

visa_types.processing_time:
Applications for a Schengen (short-term) visa can be filed within 180 days preceding the planned trip. Applications are in most cases reviewed within 15 calendar days. In individual cases,
                                                                        the review period can be extended up to 45 days and more at the discretion of the Embassy of Germany for any reason. It is recommended not to file a visa application later than 15
                                                                        days before the trip, as it cannot be otherwise guaranteed it will be reviewed in time.
The Visa Application Centre has no right to influence anyhow the processing time of visa applications as well as to accelerate the application.
Please note that you may be required to appear before a visa officer for a personal interview at the Consulate of Germany prior to a decision being taken on your application. The Embassy
                                                                        also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Applicants are requested to read the information on this site carefully. The
                                                                        guidelines listed on this website help you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy of Germany in Bangkok.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Every applicant, including children and infants, has to submit her/his Schengen visa application by appointment only on their appointment date, which needs to be booked online (see Schedule
                                                                        an Appointment) by the applicant.
Applicants traveling to Germany to visit family or friends may apply for this visa.
Schengen visas can be single, dual, or multiple-entry, valid up to five years. The validity period and number of entries are determined on a case-by-case basis by the consular mission, depending on the purpose of travel and the documents submitted in the visa.

- Colour photo on a white background

                                                                        sample requirements. 

## Travel Medical Insurance
A medical insurance is compulsory for visitors to Germany and other Schengen states. You may provide either a policy from an insurance company based in Europe or in Thailand. Please note
                                                                        that only certain insurance companies in Thailand are accepted. The insurance must cover at least 30 000 Euro and include costs for medical treatment, hospitalization and repatriation
                                                                        due to injury or medical reasons. The insurance must be valid in all Schengen states and cover the total length of the visit.
Please note, the Insurance policy will be considered valid, only if it has the insurance company stamp and authorized signature.
Click here for the list of accredited Travel Medical Insurance.

## Processing Time
Applications for a Schengen (short-term) visa can be filed within 180 days preceding the planned trip. Applications are in most cases reviewed within 15 calendar days. In individual cases,
                                                                        the review period can be extended up to 45 days and more at the discretion of the Embassy of Germany for any reason. It is recommended not to file a visa application later than 15
                                                                        days before the trip, as it cannot be otherwise guaranteed it will be reviewed in time.
The Visa Application Centre has no right to influence anyhow the processing time of visa applications as well as to accelerate the application.
Please note that you may be required to appear before a visa officer for a personal interview at the Consulate of Germany prior to a decision being taken on your application. The Embassy
                                                                        also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Applicants are requested to read the information on this site carefully. The
                                                                        guidelines listed on this website help you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy of Germany in Bangkok.

visa_types.name: Schengen Visa
visa_types.overview:
(For a maximum stay of 90 days per 180-day period)
If you wish to visit Germany for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Germany.
A short-stay Schengen visa allows the holder to transit through or remain in German territory and all other Schengen States for a maximum period of three months (90 days) within a period of 180 days
                                                            from the entry date into the Schengen Area.
Short-stay Schengen visa applications cannot be submitted more than 180 days in advance before the intended travel date.
However, they must be submitted at least 15 calendar days prior to the proposed date of travel. VFS Global is the outsourcing office responsible for the reception and release of passports for short-stay
                                                            Schengen visa applications.

visa_types.name: National Visa
visa_types.overview:
A national visa is intended for a person wishing to enter Germany for a stay of more than 90 days within a 180-day period.
The decision to be made for a national visa is subject to the approval by the competent Aliens’ Office in Germany.
The following types are falling in the category of a national visa: reunification with spouse or partner (after marriage in Thailand), marriage or partnership registration in Germany, family reunification with minor German child, immigration of children to Germany, long-term study purposes and Nursing Staff are apply with VFS Global by make an appointment here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/tha/th/deu/book-an-appointment)
For another Long term visa did not mention as above such as visa for specialty cooks, adoption, commencement of work in Germany, au-pair programs, language courses, search for employment, EU Blue Card. Please apply and make an appointment with German Embassy Directly. You can see more detail by click here (https://bangkok.diplo.de/th-th/service/visa-einreise/national-visa/1353050)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Every applicant, including children and infants, has to submit her/his Schengen visa application by appointment only on their appointment date, which needs to be booked online (see Schedule an Appointment) by the applicant.
A business visa is required if during any portion of the visit, the applicant intends to conduct business or commercial activities. e.g., meetings, conferences, trainings, trade fairs, etc. The applicant will have to provide evidence that she/he falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required.
Schengen visas can be single, dual, or multiple-entry, valid up to five years. The validity period and number of entries are determined on a case-by-case basis by the consular mission, depending on the purpose of travel and the documents submitted in the visa.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Every applicant, including children and infants, has to submit her/his Schengen visa application by appointment only on their appointment date, which needs to be booked online (see Schedule an Appointment) by the applicant.
A business visa is required if during any portion of the visit, the applicant intends to conduct business or commercial activities. e.g., meetings, conferences, trainings, trade fairs, etc. The applicant will have to provide evidence that she/he falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required.
Schengen visas can be single, dual, or multiple-entry, valid up to five years. The validity period and number of entries are determined on a case-by-case basis by the consular mission, depending on the purpose of travel and the documents submitted in the visa.

## Visa Fees
VFS Global is collecting three types of application fees:
- Visa fees, which are collected on behalf of the consular authority for the application processing.
- Application Service fees, which correspond to the standard application processing service provided by VFS Global.
- Added Value Service fees, which correspond to optional added value services you have used during the application process.
Attention: The payment of the above fees does not guarantee that a visa will be granted. The visa fee and the service fee are both non-refundable and non-transferable
                                                                        after the application is transferred to the consular authority, the visa application is denied by the administration or the applicant decides to terminate his or her application.
Effective Date 30 March 2026 

Visa fees

[Table:  Tourism]
The fee is fixed in Euro but payable in Thai Baht only.
The exchange rate between EUR and THB is decided by the Consular Authority and updated periodically. The applicable visa and service fees in THB depend on the current exchange rate, which can be changed without any notifications
The visa fee of 35 EUR is stipulated by a visa facilitation agreement with the EU for citizens of: Albania, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bosnia Herzegovina, Georgia, Macedonia, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia and Ukraine.
Visa fee exemptions
The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- Spouse, partners in civil union and underage children of German citizens
Proof by marriage certificate/birth certificate, together with German passport
- Parents of underage German citizens
Proof by birth certificate, together with German passport
- Family members of EU/EER nationals, if enjoying free movement and accompanied (in general; if they travel together with the EU/EER national or visit them)
- Children under 6 years
Application received at the Consulate before the 6th birthday, fee applies on the birthday
- School pupils, students and accompanying teachers for study or educational training
- Researchers
- Representatives of non-profit organisations/participants <25 years for non-profit events
- Trade fair exhibitors with personalized trade fair exhibitor ticket (full passport name)
- Students and accompanying teachers of PASCH schools / PASCH school exchange
- Persons officially invited by a German public institution/EU when travel and accommodation expenses are covered by German/EU/UN public funds (including scholarship holders)
- For transfer of previous multiple entry visa into new passport (old passport no free pages)
- The visa fee is also waived for official passport travelers (applying through MoFA, not VAC)
- Diplomatic and Service Passports
- UN staff/delegates and participants of UN conferences
- Seminars sponsored and organized by GIZ
Please note that the visa fees are as above indicated. No other amount will be requested for the visa issuance or for urgent process for visa issuance.
Application service fee
The below service fee is mandatory for all visa applications and is inclusive of taxes. The service fee is to be paid for every visa application. This fee corresponds to the standard
                                                                        application processing service provided by VFS Global (i.e., information provision, appointment booking, visa application reception, data processing, etc.).

[Table:  Tourism]

[Table:  Tourism]
Value Added Service fees
Please note that Value Added Services can be bought at the sole discretion of the applicant and do not have any effect on the visa decision. For more information, please check the Value
                                                                        Added Service page.
Note:
An official receipt should be obtained for all payments made.
Kindly be informed that the fees are non-refundable and non-transferable once the payment is made.

## Documents Required
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your visa application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                                                        documents.
                                                                    
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/tha/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Documents Checklist (English) – Schengen Business Visa
Dokumentencheckliste (Deutsch) – Schengenvisum für geschäftliche Zwecke

Important note: Please arrange your documents in the following order.
In order to avoid a delay when applying for your visa, please arrange your documents according to the following order.
- Copy of your passport
- Application form with photographs
- Formal obligation letter or written invitation letter / Business invitation from Germany / Hotel reservations
- Flight reservation (only for tourist visa applicants)
- All further documents, e.g., work certificate, bank account, birth certificate, land title deed, etc.
- Travel and health insurance

## Photo Specifications
Please submit two passport photos which should fulfill the following criteria (conform to ICAO standards).
The photos are part of the biometric data, mandatory for a Schengen visa application.
- Colour photo on a white background
- Size: 3.5 x 4.5cm
- Taken from the front with the subject looking directly at the camera
- Mouth must be closed, teeth not visible
- Without shadows in the face
- Wearer of glasses: the eyes must not be covered by the frames of the glasses
- Sharp, not blurred, high pixel resolution (600 DPI)
- Face covers 70-80% of the photo
- Recent photo, not older than 6 months
The photo will be imprinted into the visa sticker and must fulfill these criteria. The photo is stored in different databases (central register for foreigners, visa information system),
                                                                        so that a comparison can take place when entering the Schengen area.
Photos which are older than 6 months are not accepted by the visa section and applications containing photographs that are older than 6 months will be rejected and not processed. In order
                                                                        to avoid this, we advise you to take new photos right before the visa application submission.
Please click here (https://bangkok.diplo.de/blob/1381788/7db25b57b6280faee3494026dadca5d7/fotomustertafel-data.pdf) to see the photograph
                                                                        sample requirements. 

## Travel Medical Insurance
A medical insurance is compulsory for visitors to Germany and other Schengen states. You may provide either a policy from an insurance company based in Europe or in Thailand. Please note
                                                                        that only certain insurance companies in Thailand are accepted. The insurance must cover at least 30 000 Euro and include costs for medical treatment, hospitalization and repatriation
                                                                        due to injury or medical reasons. The insurance must be valid in all Schengen states and cover the total length of the visit.
Please note, the Insurance policy will be considered valid, only if it has the insurance company stamp and authorized signature.
Click here for the list of accredited Travel Medical Insurance.

## Processing Time
Applications for a Schengen (short-term) visa can be filed within 180 days preceding the planned trip. Applications are in most cases reviewed within 15 calendar days. In individual cases,
                                                                        the review period can be extended up to 45 days and more at the discretion of the Embassy of Germany for any reason. It is recommended not to file a visa application later than 15
                                                                        days before the trip, as it cannot be otherwise guaranteed it will be reviewed in time.
The Visa Application Centre has no right to influence anyhow the processing time of visa applications as well as to accelerate the application.
Please note that you may be required to appear before a visa officer for a personal interview at the Consulate of Germany prior to a decision being taken on your application. The Embassy
                                                                        also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Applicants are requested to read the information on this site carefully. The
                                                                        guidelines listed on this website help you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy of Germany in Bangkok.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Every applicant, including children and infants, has to submit her/his Schengen visa application by appointment only on their appointment date, which needs to be booked online (see Schedule an Appointment) by the applicant.
Applicants, who intend to travel purely for tourist purposes, i.e., visiting for leisure, recreation or sightseeing, and provide for their own accommodation (hotel, hostel, B&B, camping) need to apply as tourists.
Schengen visas can be single, dual, or multiple-entry, valid up to five years. The validity period and number of entries are determined on a case-by-case basis by the consular mission, depending on the purpose of travel and the documents submitted in the visa.

Photos which are older than 6 months are not accepted by the visa section and applications containing photographs that are older than 6 months will be rejected and not processed. In order
                                                                        to avoid this, we advise you to take new photos right before the visa application submission.

## Processing Time
As long-term visas can only be issued after approval by the competent Aliens’ Office in Germany and due to long mailing times, applications take two months on average.

## Photo Specifications
Please submit two passport photos which should fulfill the following criteria (conform to ICAO standards).
The photos are part of the biometric data, mandatory for a Schengen visa application.
- Colour photo on a white background
- Size: 3.5 x 4.5cm
- Taken from the front with the subject looking directly at the camera
- Mouth must be closed, teeth not visible
- Without shadows in the face
- Wearer of glasses: the eyes must not be covered by the frames of the glasses
- Sharp, not blurred, high pixel resolution (600 DPI)
- Face covers 70-80% of the photo
- Recent photo, not older than 6 months
The photo will be imprinted into the visa sticker and must fulfill these criteria. The photo is stored in different databases (central register for foreigners, visa information system),
                                                                        so that a comparison can take place when entering the Schengen area.
Photos which are older than 6 months are not accepted by the visa section and applications containing photographs that are older than 6 months will be rejected and not processed. In order
                                                                        to avoid this, we advise you to take new photos right before the visa application submission.

## Processing Time
Processing time may vary from 15 days to 2 months, depending on the individual application and whether a pre-approval of German authorities (Bundesagentur für Arbeit) is being submitted and whether additional approvals are needed.
